data.body: Plan-review PASSED (all advisory, no BLOCK; attestation signed @6a28e9a5a). Advisory triage: The T8/E2 findings ('WS1 never names the sub-answers', 'WS3 never names criterion ids/exec/applies_at', 'WS9 cohort field type/schema', 'WS1-10 are compound labels needing an external doc') all concern the EPIC being a thin coordination/index ticket — by design, per the guiding principle 'criterion-specific logic lives in child rubrics/tickets, not the coordinating parent'. Each detail the findings ask for is fully specified in the corresponding CHILD story (verified: every child carries file_impact + typed ACs + baked design decisions from session brief-bride-rash). Genuinely-actionable coaching is folded into child implementation, not the epic text (editing the epic now would stale the fresh attestation): (1) WS9 — record cohort backward-compat policy (missing=unknown) as a docs schema note [already in WS9 AC]; (2) WS5 — add a recall/precision eval case per rubric enrichment (T3/G3/T5c/T11/E2/G4), not only E6; (3) WS3 — note the structural alternative (reuse existing overlay_routing:llm T10/T11 path vs a new value) in the ticket [already decided: reuse]; (4) WS4/WS5 lexicon ownership — deliberately in-prompt (rubric strings), the rebar architecture choice, not config-driven. AC2 (link design/placeholder epics) is already satisfied by the epic's 4 relates_to links (grumpy-gland-rose/only-crave-art/fort-wisp-wren/spin-cub-usher). AC3/SC1 'all 11 WS complete' is the multi-session epic-completion criterion, satisfied at close, not in one session.
